The [[Slayn & Korpil/Legends|Slayn & Korpil]] '''AHS-1 heavy assault airspeeder''' was developed shortly after the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] when they realized that they had little in the way to combat the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire's]] [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]] [[Walker/Legends|walkers]].

==Characteristics==
During field trials, the craft gained the nickname "'''Walker Buster"''' due to its intended use. The prototype for this craft was created from a damaged [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter/Legends|B-wing]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]], and subsequent models followed the same general design. The AHS-1 had two of the B-wing's airfoils joined onto the B-wing's command pod. [[Repulsorlift/Legends|Repulsorlift]] engines with additional [[ion afterburner]]s were mounted on the underside of the command pod, at the rear. An additional stabilizer airfoil was mounted above and aft of the command pod and was connected to the vehicle by two vertical stabilizers which were mounted on the main airfoils.

==Armaments==
The Walker Buster's armament consisted of two [[Taim & Bak/Legends|Taim & Bak]] [[KL5 laser cannon]]s which were mounted individually on each wingtip, the wingtips hosting the same housings which also held the landing gear, as well as an [[Arakyd Industries/Legends|Arakyd]] [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedo]] launcher mounted in the rear of the command pod and fired through a tube which ran to the end of the command pod's "chin". Finally, there was a [[Thunderbolt 595 quad blaster cannon]] mounted on the nose, providing low-level firepower for the speeder's less durable targets. However, the Walker Buster's primary weapon were its four [[GX-15 concussion missile|GX-15 "Starbolt" concussion missiles]], a new form of advanced weapon produced specifically for the AHS-1.

Although its armament made it a formidable enough fighting machine, the Walker Buster had a serious drawback: its size and weight. In fact, it was so heavy that it relied as much on its airfoils for lift and stability as it did on its repulsorlifts, and even required a brief takeoff run to generate enough lift to gain altitude. Additionally, it had rather poor maneuverability. Though its ion afterburners gave it enough speed to far outperform the AT-AT and [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST]] walkers it was designed to take down, this lack of maneuverability made it particularly vulnerable to [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighter]] attack or concentrated laser fire.

==History==
Despite these flaws, the Walker Buster was expected to play a large part in the war against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] and was thought to be deployed soon after its test flights.
